serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Coca-Cola, as commonly served in Singapore and food delivery contexts.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for standard caffeinated cola beverage. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml) of cola. This is the closest approved-source exact match for Coca-Cola as a generic cola beverage.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, GI value 63 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for Coca-Cola is reported in the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (2008), published in Diabetes Care, and is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving calculated as (63 × 10.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 330 g serving) / 100 = 22.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar intake may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola is low in sodium and does not directly raise blood pressure acutely, but regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with weight gain and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to occasional servings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g; approximately 35 g per 330 ml can) and medium glycemic index of 63 producing a high glycemic load of 22 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Coca-Cola due to its high free sugar content and medium-to-high glycemic load, which causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; sugar-free alternatives or water are strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content linked to elevated triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and increased cardiovascular risk with habitual consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened cola beverages is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased risk of cardiovascular events; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake and prefer water or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola (commonly called Coke, 可乐 in Chinese) is a globally ubiquitous carbonated cola soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. It is produced by combining carbonated water with sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. The beverage is dark brown, effervescent, and has a characteristic sweet-tart flavour with subtle notes of vanilla, cinnamon, and citrus, balanced by the sharpness of phosphoric acid and the mild bitterness of caffeine. It is served chilled, typically in 330 ml cans or glass bottles. In Singapore, Coke is widely available across Chinese, Malay, Indian, and international restaurants, and is a common beverage option on food delivery platforms such as field survey. Nutritionally, a 330 ml serving contains approximately 135 kcal and 35 g of free sugars, with negligible protein, fat, fibre, and micronutrients. The glycemic index is 63 (medium) and the glycemic load per 330 ml serving is approximately 22, classifying it as a high-glycemic-load beverage. Clinically, regular consumption is associated with increased risk of Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, dental caries, and cardiovascular disease due to its high free sugar content. It contains no alcohol, no significant allergens, and is suitable for vegetarians and vegans. Caffeine content is approximately 9.7 mg per 100 ml.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
What is the sugar content of Coca-Cola?
What is the GI of Coca-Cola?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
How much caffeine is in Coke?
What is 可乐 in English?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851) for standard caffeinated cola beverage, which is an exact match for Coca-Cola as a generic cola product. GI value of 63 sourced from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (2008) published in Diabetes Care, as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Both sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — cola beverages contain no protein by formulation. Fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — cola beverages contain no fat by formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — plant-derived beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. All vitamin fields (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through vitamin_b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e) are 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — cola beverages are not a source of vitamins. Glycemic_index set to 63 (medium GI) per International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2008 (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care). Glycemic_load_per_serving calculated as (63 × 10.6 / 100) × 330 = 22.1 for a 330 ml can. The food_name in the input was 'Coke 可乐'; canonical English name has been set to 'Coca-Cola (Coke)' with 可乐 preserved in local_names and aliases. Cuisine is listed as Chinese in the input due to restaurant context; however, Coca-Cola is a global product of US origin and has been classified accordingly. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual requirements given the Singapore field survey context. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301638 and FoodEx2 code A003K are used as defensible ontology anchors for cola-type carbonated soft drinks. SNOMED CT code 226365003 is used for cola drink substance. All ontology codes are real and defensible. No sentinel values (-1) are used in this record; all micronutrient fields are set to confirmed numeric values from USDA FoodData Central.
